summary: Authorized recon previously identified multiple open services within 10.20.0.0/24, including HTTP services on 80, 8000, and 8080, SSH on 22, rpcbind on 111, and an unidentified service on 3000.
Fingerprint evidence is sufficient to support bounded follow-on analysis for the HTTP-exposed services, especially Apache on 80, Jetty 9.2.11.v20150529 on 8080, and Uvicorn on 8000.

Current snapshot indicates HTTP services likely warrant vulnerability analysis, but lacks concrete per-service banners, headers, paths, or versions needed to record defensible VulnerabilityCandidate objects without speculation.
No specific vulnerability candidate is recorded from the supplied snapshot because available evidence does not support asserting a product-specific issue or CVE with acceptable rigor.

This recon stage can run in principle under policy, but the supplied context shows the assigned authorized DMZ recon objective was already completed in a prior recon stage and no narrower new recon target or gap is specified.
A new recon tool call would likely duplicate prior evidence or repeat known failures, so finishing partial without additional probing is more defensible than inventing or re-running discovery.
